title: Puerto Banus Dolphin Watching Boat Hire: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Puerto Banus, on the Costa del Sol, offers some of the best dolphin watching in the Mediterranean, with trips departing from the marina from around EUR 80 per person for a shared tour. BoatHire24 connects you with trusted local operators to find the perfect boat.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most dolphin watching trips depart from Puerto Banus Marina, the main harbour lined with shops and restaurants. Nearby Puerto de la Duquesa and Estepona are also launching points for dolphin tours. Further along the coast, Marbella Harbour and Cabopino Port offer alternative departures. The trips often head towards the Bay of Gibraltar or the waters off Punta de Calaburras, where dolphins are frequently spotted.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor yacht, ideal for private groups up to 12 guests; from EUR 1,200 for a half-day. Consider this motor yacht for a comfortable experience.
  2. Catamaran, stable and spacious for up to 20 guests; from EUR 1,800 for a full-day trip.
  3. Rib, fast and nimble for up to 8 guests; from EUR 400 for a two-hour dolphin chase.
  4. Sunseeker, luxury style for up to 10 guests; from EUR 1,500 for a half-day.
  5. Astondoa, elegant and well-equipped for up to 12 guests; from EUR 1,100 for a half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat for dolphin watching in Puerto Banus?

Prices vary from EUR 80 per person on a shared tour to over EUR 3,000 for a private crewed yacht for the day. Here is a typical breakdown:

  • Shared dolphin tour: EUR 80, 120 per person, 2, 3 hours
  • Small motorboat half-day: EUR 600, 900 for up to 6 guests
  • Catamaran full-day: EUR 1,800, 3,000 for up to 20 guests
  • Crewed yacht (e.g. Azimut 50): EUR 2,500, 4,000 for up to 12 guests

To find the best deal, browse boats for your dates on BoatHire24 and compare verified listings.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

No licence is required for charters with a skipper, but for bareboat rental you need a valid ICC or equivalent. Most dolphin watching trips include a skipper, so you can relax. Always check with the operator on BoatHire24 for specific requirements.

Tip: Ask the skipper to tune into local radio channels for real-time dolphin sightings, the pod near Punta de Calaburras is active from May to September.

The high season runs from June to August, when the levante wind can pick up, so morning trips are often calmer. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 80, 4,000 per trip
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Trip length: 2 to 8 hours
  • Capacity: 6 to 20 guests
  • Currency: EUR
  • Distance: 5 nautical miles offshore to dolphin zones

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I see dolphins from a small motorboat?

Yes, small motorboats can get close to pods, but large yachts offer more comfort for families.

What is the best time of day for dolphin watching?

Early morning trips often have calmer seas and more dolphin activity.

Are meals included on dolphin watching charters?

Most private charters offer catering options, from tapas to full BBQ meals.

How many dolphins can I expect to see?

Common sightings include groups of 10 to 50 common or striped dolphins, especially near the Bay of Gibraltar.

Whether you are a family or a group of friends, hiring a boat for dolphin watching in Puerto Banus is an unforgettable experience. Boat owners can also browse the listings to list their own boat and reach thousands of renters. For personalised recommendations, visit BoatHire24 and compare options today.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
